stories.headline: Alaska DNR's preliminary nod to Stak Energy's 715-acre North Slope gas-fired AI data center draws a public revolt
stories.one_line: Alaska DNR issued a May 12, 2026 preliminary decision to lease Stak Energy 715.4 acres near Deadhorse for a gas-fired AI data center; the public comment window (extended to July 17) drew 500+ comments, fewer than a dozen in support.

claims.text: Alaska DNR's Division of Oil and Gas issued a preliminary decision dated May 12, 2026 regarding the Stak Energy lease (ADL 422741).
The decision would grant STAK Energy Corporation a 50-year lease of 715.4 acres of state land.

sources.quote_or_basis: 50-year lease for 715.4 acres; applicant STAK Energy Corporation
approximately 715.4 acres of state land; 50-year negotiated lease of state land

claims.text: The site is located adjacent to the Dalton Highway approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse.

sources.quote_or_basis: adjacent to the Dalton Highway approximately 26 miles south of Deadhorse

claims.notes: News outlets round to 25 miles; DNR says 26. '1 mile west' dropped.

claims.text: The site is near Dalton Highway Milepost 390.
The proposed use is a commercial natural-gas-powered energy facility including modular high-performance computing (AI data center) units.

sources.quote_or_basis: construction, operation, and maintenance of a commercial natural gas-powered energy facility, including modular high-performance computing units

claims.text: The project proposes 1 to 3 gigawatts of on-site natural-gas-fired generation.
claims.suggested_phrasing: according to the Northern Center's reading of the lease documents

sources.quote_or_basis: 1 to 3 gigawatts of on-site natural gas-fired generation

claims.notes: ADN corroborates 'a gigawatt or more.' 'Behind the meter' not verified verbatim.

claims.text: The facility would consume 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of gas per day, roughly 128 to 183 billion cubic feet per year.

sources.quote_or_basis: 350 to 500 million standard cubic feet of gas per day; 128 to 183 billion cubic feet per year

claims.text: Reported cost is about $500 million for initial site development and more than $10 billion for the total project.

sources.quote_or_basis: approximately $500 million in site evaluation, engineering, gravel road and pad construction

claims.text: Site work includes a principal gravel pad of approximately 640 acres, a 1.8-mile gravel access road, and roughly 7.1 million cubic yards of gravel fill.
claims.suggested_phrasing: according to the Northern Center's review of lease documents

sources.quote_or_basis: principal gravel pad of approximately 640 acres; 1.8-mile gravel access road; roughly 7.1 million cubic yards of gravel fill

claims.text: The public comment window originally closed at 4:30 p.m. Alaska Daylight Time on June 15, 2026, and was extended to July 17, 2026 due to comment volume.
claims.value: closed 4:30 p.m. ADT; June 15 extended to July 17, 2026

sources.quote_or_basis: 4:30 PM Alaska Daylight Time on June 15, 2026
Comment deadline extended to July 17 (original mid-June)

claims.notes: Primary notice shows original June 15; extension to July 17 reported by Northern Journal and ADN.

claims.text: More than 500 public comments were received, with fewer than a dozen in support of the project.

sources.quote_or_basis: More than 500 public comments; Fewer than a dozen comments endorsed the project
500+ total; fewer than a dozen supportive

claims.text: Stak Chief Strategy Officer John Boyle, a former Alaska DNR commissioner, said the vast majority of comments are form letters lacking substance.

sources.quote_or_basis: Our initial assessment is that the vast majority of the comments are form letters lacking substance other than reflecting an individual's point of view, - John Boyle, Stak's chief strategy officer and former DNR commissioner

claims.notes: Named-person quote confirmed verbatim on the fetched Northern Journal page.

claims.text: The generators could produce a gigawatt or more, roughly 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid.
claims.suggested_phrasing: about 30% more than urban Alaska's entire peak grid demand (per ADN); avoid citing a specific 600 MW Railbelt figure

sources.quote_or_basis: generators could produce a gigawatt or more of power, which is some 30% more than the peak demand of urban Alaska's entire grid

claims.notes: The relative comparison is verified; do NOT cite a specific 600 MW Railbelt figure (unsourced).

unverifiable_dropped: About 1 mile west of the Dalton Highway near Milepost 390 (the '1 mile west' offset unverified; DNR says 'adjacent')
Only written commenters may appeal the final finding (not found verbatim on any readable page)
Railbelt peak 'on the order of 600 MW' (no readable page states this specific figure; only the relative ~30% is sourced)
Exact 128-183 Bcf/year and 350-500 MMscf/day rest on a single advocacy-org source (northern.org)
stories.alaska_teeth: Extremely concrete and well-verified: Alaska state land on the North Slope, a preliminary decision from Alaska DNR, an Alaska public-comment process, and an Alaska grid comparison, corroborated by a primary state notice plus multiple independent Alaska outlets.

stories.headline: Shinkei Systems' computer-vision robot performs automated ike jime at sea
stories.reason_not_selected: In-window July 22 Alaska-sockeye peg unverified (403/paywall); all tech numbers trace to a single background-dated source (The Spoon, Dec 2025, CEO-attributed); no readable page proves the robot operates on Alaska boats/waters (Shinkei only SOURCES Alaska fish, processed in Tacoma). Fails the in-window + concrete-Alaska-impact bar for a lead deck. Parked as a future-update candidate if an Alaska-deployment source surfaces.
stories.alaska_teeth: Moderate/weak: sources Alaska fish; fleet ranges 'Alaska down to California'; robot NOT verified to operate in Alaska; processing in Tacoma, WA.

checker_notes: Both stories reached >=3 verified claims. STAK is the better-supported story (primary DNR notice + independent AK outlets + verbatim Boyle quote) and is SELECTED. Shinkei's core tech traces to one background-dated CEO-attributed source and its in-window/Alaska-deployment claims could not be verified (403/paywall). Paywall/403: undercurrentnews.com, intrafish.com (Zephr SSO), alaskabeacon.com.
